constraint.value: Personal data must be processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject.
Personal data must be collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes and must not be further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes.
Personal data must be adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed.
The controller must be able to demonstrate that the data subject has consented to processing of his or her personal data.

action.value: demonstrate data subject consent to processing of personal data

constraint.value: Processing of special categories of personal data is prohibited.

condition.value: The personal data reveal racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, genetic data, biometric data for unique identification, health data, sex life data, or sexual orientation data.

action.value: process special categories of personal data

constraint.value: The person is classified as subject to unlimited income tax liability.

condition.value: The person has a residence or habitual abode in the country.

action.value: be subject to unlimited income tax liability

constraint.value: The unlimited tax liability extends to all domestic and foreign income.

condition.value: The person has a domicile or habitual abode in the country.

action.value: be subject to unlimited tax liability

constraint.value: The limited tax liability extends only to the income listed in Section 98.

condition.value: The person has neither a residence nor a habitual abode in the country.

action.value: be subject to limited tax liability

constraint.value: The withdrawal of consent does not affect the lawfulness of processing based on consent before its withdrawal.
The processing is lawful.

condition.value: The processing is necessary for the purposes of the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or a third party, and such interests are not overridden by the interests or fundamental rights and freedoms of the data subject.

constraint.value: The prohibition on processing special categories of personal data does not apply.

exception.value: Union or Member State law provides that the prohibition may not be lifted by the data subject.

condition.value: The data subject has given explicit consent to the processing for one or more specified purposes.

action.value: process special categories of personal data

constraint.value: The controller must provide the data subject with the identity and contact details of the controller, the contact details of the data protection officer, the purposes of the processing, and the legal basis for the processing.

action.value: provide required information to the data subject

constraint.value: Appropriate technical and organisational measures must be implemented by the controller both at the time of the determination of the means for processing and at the time of the processing itself in order to effectively implement data protection principles.

action.value: implement appropriate technical and organisational measures

constraint.value: The record of processing activities must be maintained under the entity's responsibility and must contain the name and contact details of the controller, the purposes of the processing, a description of the categories of data subjects and categories of personal data, and, where applicable, transfers of personal data to a third country.

Personal data is defined as any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person who can be identified directly or indirectly by reference to an identifier such as a name, an identification number, location data, or an online identifier.
The annual income tax return must be submitted to the competent tax office by the end of April of the following calendar year.

condition.value: The total annual income of the taxpayer exceeds the statutory threshold.

constraint.value: Married couples are permitted to opt for joint assessment of their income tax.

condition.value: Both spouses have unlimited tax liability and do not live permanently separated.

action.value: opt for joint assessment of income tax

constraint.value: Personal living expenses of the taxpayer or the taxpayer's family members must not be deducted from taxable income under any circumstances.

action.value: deduct personal living expenses from taxable income

constraint.value: The income tax exemption applies to employment income up to an annual amount of fifteen thousand euros.

exception.value: The taxpayer voluntarily opts for standard assessment.

condition.value: The taxpayer has no other sources of income and the total annual income does not exceed fifteen thousand euros.

action.value: exclude employment income from income tax

constraint.value: Taxable income must be computed as the total amount of income from all seven income categories after deduction of special expenses, extraordinary burdens, and tax allowances, and the resulting amount must be rounded down to whole euros.
The income tax assessment notice must be issued in writing and contain the amount of taxable income, the applicable tax rate, the computed tax liability, and any tax credits applied.
The applicable tax rate must be fifty-five percent for the portion of income exceeding one million euros.

exception.value: The taxpayer has claimed extraordinary tax relief measures in the same assessment period.

condition.value: The taxpayer's total annual income exceeds one million euros.

constraint.value: Business expenses encompass any expenditures or outlays meeting the specified criteria, such as material costs, personnel expenses, fixed asset depreciation, and business loan interest.

condition.value: Expenditures or outlays are incurred through business operations and have an objective connection to the business activity.

constraint.value: Income is defined as the total amount of all revenues in money or money's worth that accrue to a person.

condition.value: The revenues accrue within a calendar year from employment, self-employment, business operations, capital assets, rental and leasing, agriculture and forestry, and other recurring benefits.
